data.title: AbbVie declares $1.73 dividend
data.description:
• AbbVie (ABBV) declares $1.73/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.04%
  • Payable May 15; for shareholders of record April 15; ex-div April 15.
  • See ABBV Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Stock markets falls ahead of a slew of economic reports, Fedspeak
data.description:
Wall Street fell on Thursday as traders faced a busy day with a slew of economic data and Fedspeak.

The S&P 500 (SP500) -0.3%, the Dow (DJI) -0.3%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -0.5% 

The Philly Fed Manufacturing Index rose in February. The U.S. trade deficit swelled more than expected in December.

The initial jobless claims fell more than expected in the past week.

Federal Reserve speakers, including Fed Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman, Minneapolis Fed Bank President Neel Kashkari, and Chicago Fed Bank President Austan Goolsbee, will speak at different events.

The Fed balance sheet will be released later in the day.

U.S. Treasury yields moved higher across the curve. The shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 3.48%, the benchmark U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) climbed 2 basis points to 4.10%, and the longer-end U.S. 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) added 1 basis point to 4.73%.

data.title: Minneapolis Fed's Kashkari sees monetary policy as 'pretty close to neutral'
data.description:
The Federal Reserve is close to achieving its dual mandate of stable prices and full employment, but is still short of both its goals, Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari said on Thursday. Inflation has receded from the highs of 2022-2023, and the unemployment rate is still relatively low at 4.3%.

Regarding the central bank's monetory policy, "my guess is we're pretty close to neutral," which means the federal funds rate level is neither stimulating nor depressing the economy, he said at the Midwest Economic Summit.

In the moderated conversation, Kashkari responded to National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett's criticisim of New York Fed research on the impact of tariffs. It's "another step to try to compromise the Fed's independence," Kashkari said. That adds to the Justice Department's criminal investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell's oversight of the Fed's multi-billion-dollar renovation project, he said.

"The louder the political noise, the more we focus on our job," he said.

Kashkari expressed skepticism about stablecoins. He said proponents of the digital currency often use “buzzword salad” when explaining its benefits. When asking about the benefits of crypto or stablecoins, "don't settle for word salad, nonsense answers," he said .

Developing… check back for updates.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to  click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Brambles Limited GAAP EPS of $0.37, revenue of $3.53B beats by $10M
data.description:
• Brambles Limited press release (BMBLF): 1H GAAP Basic EPS of $0.37.
  • Revenue of $3.53B (+4.7% Y/Y) beats by $10M.

data.title: MDA Space launches 49north defence unit, appoints Joe Armstrong as President
data.description:
• MDA Space (TSX: MDA) on Thursday said that it has launched 49North, a wholly owned subsidiary focused on delivering multi-domain and mission-critical capabilities for Canada’s national defence priorities outside the space domain.
  • The new Ottawa-based unit will provide secure C4ISR and defence systems across land, air, maritime, and joint operations, the company said.

  • MDA Space also appointed Joe Armstrong as president of 49North. Armstrong previously held senior executive roles at CAE, including chief operating officer for its defence and security business.

  • The company said 49North will focus on multi-domain integration, advanced sensing and radar technologies, autonomous systems, secure digital mission systems, and long-term in-service support.
  • MDALF +0.36% after hours to $28.0381.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Vale to sell most of Thompson Nickel Belt operations to new consortium
data.description:
Vale (VALE) said Thursday it agreed to sell most of its stake in the Thompson Nickel Belt venture in Manitoba to Exiro Minerals, Orion Resources Partners, and Canada Growth Fund as part of a strategic review of its base metals business.

Vale (VALE) said its Vale Base Metals subsidiary will retain an 18.9% minority stake as part of a new four-party consortium for the Thompson venture, with Exiro, Orion, and Canada Growth Fund collectively holding 81.1%.

The consortium partners agreed to invest as much as $200M at Thompson, while Vale Base Metals (VALE) signed an offtake agreement for concentrate produced at the Thompson mill, maintaining its status as Canada's largest nickel supplier.

The group did not disclose the terms of the transaction, which is expected to close by year-end.

data.title: Bitdeer plans $300M private placement of notes, registered direct offering of class A shares
data.description:
Bitdeer Technologies Group (BTDR) on Thursday announced plans for a $300M private placement of convertible senior notes and registered direct offering of class A ordinary shares.

Shares were -15.92% pre-market to $8.08.

The bitcoin mining and AI infrastructure company said it intends to offer the $300M notes due 2032 to institutional buyers.

Additionally, the Singapore-based company intends to grant the initial purchasers an option to buy up to $45M principal amount of the notes.

The general, senior unsecured obligations are expected to accrue interest payable semiannually in arrears.

Upon conversion, the company said it will pay cash, class A ordinary shares, or a combination of cash and class A ordinary shares.

The interest rate, initial conversion rate, repurchase or redemption rights, and certain other terms of the notes will be determined at the time of pricing.

Additionally, Bitdeer Technologies said it intends to offer class A ordinary shares to certain holders of its 5.25% convertible senior notes due 2029.

The number of shares to be sold in the registered direct placement and the price per share will be determined at the time of pricing.

Net proceeds from both issuances are intended to be used to pay the cost of certain related capped call transactions and the cash consideration for the concurrent note repurchase transactions.

Remaining proceeds are expected to be used for datacenter expansion, HPC and AI cloud business expansion, ASIC-based mining rig development and manufacture, as well as working capital and other general corporate purposes.

data.title: ESGold to raise up to C$7 million in brokered private placement
data.description:
• ESGold (CSE: ESAU) (OTCQB: ESAUF) on Thursday said it has entered into an agreement with Red Cloud Securities Inc. to conduct a brokered “best efforts” private placement to raise gross proceeds of up to C$7.0 million.
  • The offering will consist of up to 10,295,000 units priced at C$0.68 each.
  • Each unit will include one common share and one-half of a warrant, with each full warrant exercisable at C$1.00 per share for 36 months after closing.
  • The company has also granted the agent an option to sell up to an additional 1,471,000 units for up to C$1.0 million in additional gross proceeds.
  • ESGold said it intends to use the net proceeds to advance its Montauban project in Quebec and for general working capital and corporate purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close on March 10, 2026, subject to regulatory approvals, including approval from the Canadian Securities Exchange.
  • ESAUF closed -1.03% at $0.5154.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nvidia likely to offer 'typical' upside when it reports Q4 results: Oppenheimer
data.description:
• Nvidia (NVDA) is likely to offer a “typical” upside of between $2B and $3B when it reports fourth-quarter results, investment firm Oppenheimer said.
  • “We see 'typical' $2B-$3B upside potential to Street F4Q (Jan.)/F1Q (Apr.) sales $65.6B/$71.6B led by GB300 Ultra,” analyst Rick Schafer wrote in a note to clients. “[Cloud service provider] capex continues to increase. Expected $650B in CY26 vs. >$400B in CY25. Frontier LLM size growing 10x/year. Reasoning tokens growing >5x. NVDA rack-scale NVL72 remains the clear leader in AI performance/watt. Vera Rubin (VR200) on track for volume/production ramp F3Q, followed by VR300 Ultra early 3Q27.”
  • Delving deeper, Schafter said the average selling price for Vera Rubin is likely to be between 40% and 50% above that of GB300, which sold for roughly $3.5M. As such, Vera Rubin could add roughly $8B in revenue to Nvidia, Schafer added. 
  • Additionally, with China now in the mix (representing a possible $50B total addressable market), that could boost Nvidia's total addressable market even higher, now estimated to be around $4T, Schafer said. “NVDA is the ubiquitous AI platform best positioned to win, in our view,” the analyst wrote.
  • Schafer reiterated his Outperform rating and $265 target on Nvidia.

data.title: Bitcoin Depot to implement 1-for-7 reverse stock split on Feb. 23
data.description:
• Bitcoin Depot (BTM) will effect a 1-for-7 reverse stock split of its Class A common stock effective February 23, 2026.
  • Stockholders previously authorized a reverse split range of 1-for-5 to 1-for-20, with the board approving the 1-for-7 ratio on February 12, 2026.
  • Warrants will be proportionately adjusted, with the post-split exercise price increasing to $80.50/share.
  • BTM shares down 5.5% premarket.
